zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Neville於大約1356年出生在英國藍卡爾宏比 卡素時，她的父親Sir Robert Neville III35歲，母親Margaret de la Pole31歲。她於大約1401年4月17日與Sir William Harington of Farleton結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1439年12月30日在她的家鄉享年84歲，埋葬在英國藍卡爾梅林。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret Neville于大约1356年出生在英国藍卡爾宏比 卡素时，她的父亲Sir Robert Neville III35岁，母亲Margaret de la Pole31岁。她于大约1401年4月17日与Sir William Harington of Farleton结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1439年12月30日在她的家乡享年84岁，埋葬在英国藍卡爾梅林。

nameMeanings.name: Neville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Neuville in Calvados or Néville in Seine-Maritime, both so called from Old French neu(f) ‘new’ (from Latin novus) + ville ‘settlement’. One family line, originally from Neuville (Calvados), became powerful during the Wars of the Roses through Richard Neville (1428–71), 16th Earl of Warwick, nicknamed "The Kingmaker".
Irish (Munster): assimilation of the Gaelic name Ó Niadh (see Nee ) and sometimes of Ó Cnaimhín (see Nevin ).

History: George Neville came to VA in or c. 1700 and settled on the headwaters of the Occoquan River, acquiring a large estate. His descendants, bearing the surnames Neville and Craig, were of considerable importance in Pittsburgh, PA, and Cincinnati, OH.

meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

meanings: English and Scottish (Wigtownshire): of Norman origin, a variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.
Dutch and North German: unexplained.

variants.name: Nee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, John Patrick.

Irish (Galway): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Niadh ‘descendant of Nia’ or Mac Niadh ‘son of Nia’. Compare McNee . Needham 2 and Neville have also been adopted as Anglicized forms of this name.
North German: possibly a relationship name from Middle Low German neve ‘nephew, grand-child’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Neville was born about 1356, in Hornby Castle, Lancashire, England, her father, Sir Robert Neville III, was 35 and her mother, Margaret de la Pole, was 31. She married Sir William Harington of Farleton about 17 April 1401. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 30 December 1439, in her hometown, at the age of 84, and was buried in Melling, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret Neville est née environ 1356, à Hornby Castle, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert Neville III, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Margaret de la Pole, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Harington of Farleton environ 17 avril 1401. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 30 décembre 1439, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterrée à Melling, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaret Neville um 1356 in Hornby Castle, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert Neville III 35 und ihre Mutter Margaret de la Pole 31. Sie hat um 17. April 1401 Sir William Harington of Farleton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 30. Dezember 1439 im Alter von 84 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Melling, Lancashire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Neville è nata il circa 1356 nel Hornby Castle, Lancashire, England, sua padre, Sir Robert Neville III, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Margaret de la Pole, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Sir William Harington of Farleton circa 17 aprile 1401. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 30 dicembre 1439, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 84 anni, e fu sepolta a Melling, Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Nevilleが約1356年にイギリスランカシュアイアホールンビー カストルで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Neville IIIは35歳，母Margaret de la Poleは31歳であった。約1401年4月17日にSir William Harington of Farletonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1439年12月30日に故郷で84歳で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアメリングにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Neville(이)가 대략 1356년 영국 랭카실르 호른비 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Neville III(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de la Pole(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 대략 1401년 4월 17일 Sir William Harington of Farleton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1439년 12월 30일 그녀의 고향에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 멜링에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Neville nasceu aproximadamente 1356, em Hornby Castle, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert Neville III, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de la Pole, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William Harington of Farleton aproximadamente 17 de abril de 1401. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 30 de dezembro de 1439, em sua cidade natal, com 84 anos, e foi sepultada em Melling, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Neville родилась около 1356 г. в местности хаурнби кастл, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Robert Neville III было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de la Pole, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Harington of Farleton около 17 апреля 1401 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 30 декабря 1439 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 84 лет и была похоронена в местности меллинг, ланкашир, Англия.

Se casó con Sir William Harington of Farleton aproximadamente 17 de abril de 1401. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas.
